title: Camera with dock having automated alignment
abstract: A camera integrates a magnet at one end of a housing to magnetically attract and attach the camera to a front side of a peripheral display, such as to support a video conference through an information handling system interfaced with the display. Camera position at a display dock is biased to an upright orientation by opposing polarity magnets included in the camera and the dock. The opposing polarity magnets further align a wireless charger of the dock with a wireless charger receiver of the camera to provide wireless charging of the camera from the dock.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An information handling system comprising: a processor operable to execute instructions to process information; a memory interfaced with the processor and operable to store the instructions and information; a display interfaced with the processor and operable to present the information as visual images at a display panel, the display panel having a ferromagnetic material disposed at a rear side of the display panel; a camera having a camera module disposed in one end of a cylindrical housing to capture visual images from a field of view at a front side of the cylindrical housing; a dock coupled to the display and having a curved support surface to accept the camera cylindrical housing; and opposing magnets disposed in the cylindrical housing and dock, the opposing magnets having opposing polarity configured to bias the cylindrical housing to an upright orientation. 2. The information handling system of Claim 1 further comprising: a wireless charger disposed under the curved support surface; and a wireless charger receiver included in the cylindrical housing to align with the wireless charger when the cylindrical housing has an upright orientation. 3. The information handling system of Claim 2 wherein: the opposing magnets have polarity orientation offset from the upright orientation and defining a space between the opposing magnets for the wireless charger and wireless charging receiver. 4. The information handling system of Claim 3 wherein the wireless charger and wireless charging receiver are disposed in the space between the opposing magnets. 5. The information handling system of Claim 2 further comprising: -27- 

 a magnet disposed in the cylindrical housing opposite the camera module and configured to hold the cylindrical housing in position against a display panel having a ferromagnetic backplate; and a privacy support extending up from a rear side of the dock and having a ferromagnetic material to bias the cylindrical housing towards the rear of the dock in the upright alignment. 6. The information handling system of Claim 2 wherein the opposing magnets further comprise a symmetrical arrangement of plural magnets in each of the cylindrical housing and dock. 7. The information handling system of Claim 6 wherein the opposing magnets are located at a central position of the cylindrical housing and the dock to align the wireless charger and wireless charger receiver when the camera field of view is directed towards the front and towards the rear of the display. 8. The information handling system of Claim 2 wherein the opposing magnets include a first set of opposing magnets disposed at a front face of the cylindrical housing proximate the camera module and a second set of opposing magnets disposed in a privacy support at the rear of the dock, the first and second set of opposing magnets having opposing polarity to bias the cylindrical housing to an upright orientation. 9. The information handling system of Claim 2 further comprising an alignment indicator disposed at a front side of the cylindrical housing and providing an indication of upright alignment. 10. A method for coupling a camera to a display, the method comprising: coupling a dock to the display, the dock having a curved surface; placing a cylindrical housing of the camera on the dock curved surface; and biasing the cylindrical housing to an upright position with opposing polarity magnets disposed in the cylindrical housing and dock. 11. The method of Claim 10 further comprising: 

 aligning a wireless charger of the dock and a wireless charging receiver of the cylindrical housing when the cylindrical housing has an upright orientation; and wirelessly charging the camera from the dock. 12. The method of Claim 11 further comprising: including first and second magnets of a first polarity in the cylindrical housing on opposite sides of the wireless charging receiver and offset from the upright orientation; and including first and second magnets of a second polarity in the dock on opposite sides of the wireless charging receiver and offset from the upright orientation, the first and second magnets of the first polarity cooperating with the first and second magnets of the second polarity to bias the cylindrical housing to an upright position. 13. The method of Claim 12 wherein the first and second magnets of the first polarity and the first and second magnets of the second polarity bias the cylindrical housing to the upright orientation when the cylindrical housing rests on the dock with the camera field of view forward and the camera field of view rearward. 14. The method of Claim 10 further comprising: including a main magnet in the cylindrical housing at an end opposite the camera field of view; and coupling the camera against a display panel with magnetic attraction of the main magnet against a ferromagnetic backplate of the display panel. 15. The method of Claim 14 further comprising: removing the camera from the display panel; and placing the camera in the dock with the camera field of view directed at a back support of the dock that blocks capture of visual images by the camera. 16. The method of Claim 15 further comprising: 

 detecting alignment of the wireless charger and wireless charging receiver with the camera field of view directed at the back support; and charging a battery of the camera with wireless charging signals. 17. The method of Claim 16 further comprising magnetically attracting the front side of the cylindrical housing against the back support of the dock. 18. A camera comprising: a cylindrical housing; a camera module disposed at one end of the cylindrical housing and aligned to capture visual images from a field of view; a wireless charger receiver disposed in the cylindrical housing; a dock having a curved support surface configured to hold the cylindrical housing, the dock having a wireless charger configured to provide wireless power signals to the wireless charger receiver; and first and second sets of opposing polarity magnets disposed in the cylindrical housing and the dock and configured to bias the wireless charger into alignment with the wireless charger receiver. 19. The camera of Claim 18 further comprising: a privacy support extending upward from the dock; wherein the camera rests on the curved support surface and against the privacy support both with the field of view towards and away from the privacy support to align the wireless charger and wireless charger receiver. 20. The camera of Claim 18 wherein the first and second sets of opposing polarity magnets are further disposed to align the camera module to a vertical axis for capturing a field of view normal the display.
